---
title: "Quarto 5 — Revealjs Demonstration"
subtitle: "One source pattern, a different audience"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Meta-observation

This presentation is a `.qmd` file rendered to `revealjs`.

::: {.fragment}
The instructor can open `afternoon_quarto/slides/05_revealjs_presentations.qmd` to show the source.
:::

# The core rule {.section-divider}

## Level-two headings begin slides

```markdown
## First slide

Content.

## Second slide

More content.
```

::: {.fragment}
The rest is layout and presentation design.
:::

# Useful patterns {.section-divider}

## A section divider

```markdown
# Methods {.section-divider}
```

## Incremental reveal

```markdown
::: {.incremental}
- First point
- Second point
- Third point
:::
```

## Two columns

```markdown
:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
Left.
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
Right.
:::
::::
```

## Speaker notes

```markdown
::: {.notes}
Reminder visible in speaker view.
:::
```

# Design choice {.section-divider}

## A report is not a slide deck

::: {.incremental}
- One message per slide.
- Use less text than a report.
- Enlarge labels and figures.
- Move detail to notes or a handout.
- Test the deck on the presentation computer.
:::

# Safe learner edit {.section-divider}

## Work in a copy

1. Copy `student_work/my_report.qmd` to `student_work/my_slides.qmd`.
2. Change `format: html` to `format: revealjs`.
3. Insert one `##` heading to create another slide.
4. Render the copy.
5. Leave `my_report.qmd` unchanged.

::: {.callout-tip}
This is a single edit, not a second capstone. Return to the cumulative report when the demonstration ends.
:::

## Next

The instructor demonstrates parameterized reports. The capstone follows that demonstration.
